questions.question: The ancient Indian university of Takshashila was located in which modern-day country?
questions.explanation: The ancient Indian university of Takshashila was located in modern-day Pakistan.

questions.question: Which of the following rivers is the longest in South India?
questions.explanation: The Godavari River is the longest in South India, stretching over 1,465 km.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to freedom of speech and expression?
questions.explanation: Article 19 of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to freedom of speech and expression.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme launched in 2015 to promote entrepreneurship and job creation?
questions.explanation: The government scheme launched in 2015 to promote entrepreneurship and job creation is called Start-Up India.

questions.question: Which scientist is credited with the discovery of the first antibiotic, penicillin?
questions.explanation: Alexander Fleming is credited with the discovery of the first antibiotic, penicillin, in 1928.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions and mitigating climate change?
questions.explanation: The Paris Agreement is an international agreement aimed at reducing greenhouse gas emissions and mitigating climate change.

questions.question: Who is the current Chairman of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO)?
questions.explanation: S. Somanath is the current Chairman of the Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO).

questions.question: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on which date in 1942?
questions.explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched by Mahatma Gandhi on August 8, 1942.

questions.question: Which state has the longest coastline in India?
questions.explanation: Gujarat has the longest coastline in India, stretching over 1,600 km.

questions.question: Which constitutional amendment abolished the practice of untouchability in India?
questions.explanation: Article 17 of the Indian Constitution abolished the practice of untouchability in India.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme launched in 2014 to promote financial inclusion and provide banking services to the unbanked?
questions.explanation: The government scheme launched in 2014 to promote financial inclusion and provide banking services to the unbanked is called Jan Dhan Yojana.

questions.question: Which type of rock is formed from the cooling and solidification of magma or lava?
questions.explanation: Igneous rocks are formed from the cooling and solidification of magma or lava.

questions.question: What is the name of the international convention aimed at conserving and sustainably using biodiversity?
options: Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species
United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
questions.explanation: The Convention on Biological Diversity is an international convention aimed at conserving and sustainably using biodiversity.

questions.question: Who is the current President of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB)?
questions.explanation: Jin Liqun is the current President of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank (AIIB).

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization is known for its sophisticated urban planning and architecture, with which of the following cities being a prime example?
questions.explanation: Mohenjo-Daro is a prime example of the sophisticated urban planning and architecture of the Indus Valley Civilization.

questions.question: Which mountain range runs along the border between India and Nepal?
questions.explanation: The Himalayas run along the border between India and Nepal.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to equality before the law?
questions.explanation: Article 14 of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to equality before the law.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme launched in 2015 to promote digital payments and financial inclusion?
questions.explanation: The government scheme launched in 2015 to promote digital payments and financial inclusion is called Digital India.

questions.question: Which scientist is credited with the discovery of the theory of evolution through natural selection?
questions.explanation: Charles Darwin is credited with the discovery of the theory of evolution through natural selection.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement aimed at reducing the production and consumption of ozone-depleting substances?
options: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
questions.explanation: The Montreal Protocol is an international agreement aimed at reducing the production and consumption of ozone-depleting substances.

questions.question: Who is the current Secretary-General of the United Nations?
questions.explanation: António Guterres is the current Secretary-General of the United Nations.

questions.question: The Mughal emperor Akbar established his capital at which city in 1569?
questions.explanation: The Mughal emperor Akbar established his capital at Fatehpur Sikri in 1569.

questions.question: Which river flows through the states of Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and Maharashtra?
questions.explanation: The Narmada River flows through the states of Madhya Pradesh, Gujarat, and Maharashtra.

questions.question: Which article of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion?
questions.explanation: Article 25 of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme launched in 2015 to promote skill development and entrepreneurship among youth?
questions.explanation: The government scheme launched in 2015 to promote skill development and entrepreneurship among youth is called Skill India.

questions.question: Which type of wave requires a medium to propagate and can be either longitudinal or transverse?
questions.explanation: Mechanical waves require a medium to propagate and can be either longitudinal or transverse.

questions.question: What is the name of the international convention aimed at conserving and managing the world's oceans?
options: United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea
Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species
questions.explanation: The United Nations Convention on the Law of the Sea is an international convention aimed at conserving and managing the world's oceans.

questions.question: Who is the current President of the World Bank?
questions.explanation: David Malpass is the current President of the World Bank.

questions.question: The Indian National Congress was founded in which year?
questions.explanation: The Indian National Congress was founded in 1885.

questions.explanation: Karnataka is the largest producer of coffee in India.

questions.explanation: The government scheme launched in 2014 to promote entrepreneurship and job creation in the manufacturing sector is called Make in India.

questions.question: Which scientist is credited with the discovery of the structure of DNA?
questions.explanation: James Watson and Francis Crick are credited with the discovery of the structure of DNA.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement aimed at reducing deforestation and promoting sustainable forest management?
options: United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change
Reducing Emissions from Deforestation and Forest Degradation
questions.explanation: The Reducing Emissions from Deforestation and Forest Degradation (REDD+) is an international agreement aimed at reducing deforestation and promoting sustainable forest management.

questions.question: Who is the current Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO)?
questions.explanation: Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus is the current Director-General of the World Health Organization (WHO).

questions.question: The Battle of Plassey was fought in which year?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Plassey was fought in 1757.

questions.explanation: The Rohtang Pass connects the states of Jammu and Kashmir and Himachal Pradesh.

questions.explanation: Article 19 of the Indian Constitution deals with the right to freedom of assembly.

questions.question: What is the name of the government scheme launched in 2015 to promote digital payments and financial inclusion?
questions.explanation: The government scheme launched in 2015 to promote digital payments and financial inclusion is called Digital India.

questions.question: Which type of radiation has the highest frequency and the shortest wavelength?
questions.explanation: Gamma radiation has the highest frequency and the shortest wavelength.

questions.question: What is the name of the international agreement aimed at reducing the production and consumption of persistent organic pollutants (POPs)?
questions.explanation: The Stockholm Convention is an international agreement aimed at reducing the production and consumption of persistent organic pollutants (POPs).

questions.explanation: Ursula von der Leyen is the current President of the European Commission.

questions.explanation: The Indian Rebellion of 1857 was led by Bahadur Shah II, among other leaders.

questions.explanation: The government scheme launched in 2016 to promote entrepreneurship and job creation among Scheduled Castes is called Stand-Up India.

questions.explanation: The government scheme launched in 2015 to promote entrepreneurship and job creation in the service sector is called Start-Up India.

questions.explanation: Metamorphic rocks are formed from the alteration of existing rocks under high pressure and temperature.
